hero.title: Motubrain: the World Action Model for embodied AI
hero.description: An independent bilingual guide to ShengShu Technology's Motubrain launch, benchmark claims, architecture, and current access status.
hero.tip: Independent resource. Motubrain.org is not affiliated with ShengShu Technology.

image.alt: Abstract Motubrain world action model diagram

introduce.title: What is Motubrain?
introduce.description: Motubrain is presented by ShengShu Technology as a World Action Model: a unified embodied AI model that connects what a robot sees with the actions it should take.

items.title: World Action Model
items.description: The official framing moves beyond video-only world modeling by joining perception, prediction, and robot action in one system.

items.title: Embodied AI Focus
items.description: The launch describes robots acting across homes, industrial spaces, and commercial environments rather than a consumer chatbot or memory app.

items.title: Source-Led Status
items.description: As of April 29, 2026, official pages explain the model and its benchmark claims, but do not show a public self-serve API or download.

items.title: Name Variants
items.description: Searches may use Motubrain or MotuBrain. This guide treats both as the same ShengShu World Action Model unless a source says otherwise.

benefits.title: How the World Action Model works
benefits.description: The core idea is to learn video, language, and action together so a robot can reason about what changes next and what to do next.

items.title: Video dynamics plus action
items.description: ShengShu describes Motubrain as learning video and action as continuous modalities, so world prediction and action generation are trained together rather than bolted together.

items.title: Five capabilities in one training run
items.description: The launch says one training process covers VLA control, world modeling, video generation, inverse dynamics modeling, and joint video-action prediction.

items.title: Foresight during execution
items.description: The model is positioned as anticipating environmental changes while driving actions, helping robots retry or adapt when a physical attempt does not work.

usage.title: How to read the launch claims
usage.description: Use the source trail before treating any model score as independently settled.

items.title: Start with the official page
items.description: Use ShengShu's Motubrain page for the stated capabilities, partner context, and benchmark figures.

items.title: Check benchmark mechanics
items.description: WorldArena explains EWM Score, while RoboTwin 2.0 documents the dual-arm manipulation benchmark context.

items.title: Separate access from awareness
items.description: The current public materials explain Motubrain, but this site found no official self-serve API, downloadable model, or public demo.

features.title: Why robotics teams are watching Motubrain
features.description: The launch frames Motubrain as a shift from task-specific robot systems toward scalable embodied intelligence.

items.title: One brain, many skills
items.description: Official materials say task variety improves multi-task performance instead of requiring isolated skill training for every behavior.

items.title: One brain, any robot
items.description: Motubrain is positioned as cross-embodiment, designed to adapt across robot types rather than being tied to one hardware platform.

items.title: Long-horizon execution
items.description: The model is described as learning full task sequences directly, including complex multi-step work beyond short atomic actions.

items.title: WorldArena context
items.description: WorldArena evaluates embodied world models across perceptual and functional utility, including action-planning roles.

items.title: RoboTwin 2.0 context
items.description: RoboTwin 2.0 is a large-scale dual-arm manipulation benchmark with 50 tasks and domain randomization.

items.title: Careful source posture
items.description: Benchmark numbers on this site are reported as source claims, not independent certification by Motubrain.org.

stats.title: Benchmark snapshot
stats.description: Key figures reported by official or benchmark sources as of April 29, 2026.

items.description: WorldArena EWM Score reported by ShengShu for Motubrain
RoboTwin 2.0 clean and randomized scores reported on the official page

items.title: Apr 29, 2026
items.description: Public launch date in the ShengShu PRNewswire announcement

items.question: What is Motubrain?
items.answer: Motubrain is ShengShu Technology's World Action Model for embodied robots. It is described as a unified model that connects perception, prediction, language understanding, and robot action.

items.question: Is Motubrain.org the official Motubrain website?
items.answer: No. Motubrain.org is an independent information hub. The official Motubrain page is hosted by ShengShu Technology at shengshu.com.

items.question: Can I use a Motubrain API or demo here?
items.answer: No. As of April 29, 2026, the public official materials reviewed for this site do not show a self-serve Motubrain API, downloadable model, or public demo.

items.question: What benchmark scores were announced for Motubrain?
items.answer: ShengShu reports a 63.77 EWM Score on WorldArena and RoboTwin 2.0 scores of 95.8 in the clean setting and 96.1 in the randomized setting. Treat these as source-attributed launch claims unless independent updates appear.

items.question: How is a World Action Model different from a normal world model?
items.answer: A conventional world model often focuses on predicting how a scene may evolve. Motubrain's World Action Model framing adds action generation, so the model predicts the world while producing robot actions.

items.question: Why does Vidu appear in Motubrain coverage?
items.answer: Vidu is ShengShu's video generation platform. ShengShu presents Motubrain as a robotics extension of world-model technology that grew from video simulation toward physical action.

brand.title: Motubrain.org
brand.description: Independent bilingual guide to Motubrain, the World Action Model for embodied AI. Not affiliated with ShengShu Technology.
